title: Paper 5 — The Oloid Path Carrier: A Unified Theory of Rolling Transport and Continuous Constraint Propagation
abstract: This paper proves the first path-carrier theorem: transport can be continuous without being straight-line. Paper 4 converts a registered interaction failure into a typed boundary constraint. This paper proves that such a constraint can be carried along a legal rolling path without requiring straight-line transport and without rewriting the path rule. The verified claim is affirmative and structural: legal rolling steps preserve path continuity; head/tail dyads remain binary; Paper 4 constraints can be attached as payloads; and payloads do not alter the carrier rule. The oloid path carrier is a deterministic finite-state transducer whose 8 states are the LCR carrier states and whose 8 transitions correspond to the 8 segments of the closed path around the oloid.

D_claims.claim: Rolling carrier state space q = (sheet, phase, parity) and deterministic roll(q,b) function
D_claims.evidence: Definitions 2.2–2.3 — closed-form arithmetic on finite domains

D_claims.claim: Oloid is a developable surface formed by intersection of two perpendicular unit circles (Schatz 1929)
Cayley–Dickson oloid normal form module exists in lattice_forge/
Substrate map: 8 LCR states, 8×4 transition table, depth-4096 verification
Worked trace for input [1,0,1,1,0,0,1,0] is computable and reproducible
Receipt verification items (trace_length, adjacent_pairs, head_tail_dyads, payload_noninterference) are executable checks

I_claims.claim: 8-segment LCR parameterization of the oloid path (ZERO → e3+ → C+ → FULL → C- → e1- → C0 → e2-0 → ZERO)
I_claims.reasoning: Author's structural reading; Cayley–Dickson doubling gives 2³ = 8 structure but specific oloid-path correspondence is framing

I_claims.claim: Fano plane order of the 7 non-trivial LCR states
I_claims.reasoning: Author's framing; Fano plane has 7 points and 7 lines, and the 7 LCR states can be put in bijection

I_claims.claim: Transducer noninterference theorem (repair row is side-channel)
I_claims.reasoning: Structural reading; the transducer is a finite-state machine but noninterference is a logical consequence, not a literal module

I_claims.claim: Path closure, graph continuity, and payload noninterference as oloid-path properties
I_claims.reasoning: Substrate map preservation at depth 4096 is D; specific oloid-path properties are I

I_claims.claim: Cayley–Dickson doubling of the oloid to higher dimensions (Open Problems 10.1, 10.3, 10.4)
I_claims.reasoning: Cayley–Dickson construction is D; specific 4-dimensional oloid is not constructed

I_claims.claim: Carrier action S = −m∫ds and geodesic interpretation of the oloid path
I_claims.reasoning: Natural geometric formalism but specific physical constants and minimization claim are interpretive

I_claims.claim: SM gauge structure correspondence (8 contact points → 8 SM fermion states; repair row → gauge field)
E6→E7→E8 dyadic lift as named bridge obligation
